rt1-3-1.title: Hands-on: turn ionization events into film density
rt1-3-1.intro: Film blackens because photons interact in the emulsion — every step of density is an ionization tally. Land the density window by choosing the exposure time, then hold it after 30 days of decay thins the photon supply.

tasks.label: Expose until film density lands between D 2.0 and 3.0
tasks.hint: Density is an ionization tally — exposure E = A × t, so give the emulsion more time until enough photons have interacted.

tasks.label: Jump 30 days ahead and recover the density window
tasks.hint: The decayed source fires fewer photons per minute, so stretch the time until the total interaction count A × t matches.

rt1-3-2.title: Hands-on: survey the field an 80 Ci Ir-192 source makes
rt1-3-2.intro: Exposure is ionization in air, and an ion-chamber survey meter collects exactly that charge. Survey the boundary around an 80 Ci Ir-192 device and post the line where the 2 mR/h limit truly sits — plan with the emissivity constant, believe the meter.

tasks.label: Record a stable exposure-rate reading at stakes A, B, C
tasks.hint: Hold the meter still — it is an air-filled ion chamber collecting charge; the mR/h reading only records once it settles.

tasks.label: Drag stake B out until the rate sits at the 2 mR/h limit
tasks.hint: Plan with emissivity (Ir-192 ≈ 0.5 R/h per Ci at 1 m, falling as 1/d²), but the calibrated meter — not the constant — sets the post.

rt1-3-3.title: Hands-on: beat the field with time, distance, shielding
rt1-3-3.intro: Attenuation and geometry are your only levers against a hot source. Prove the inverse square law by doubling your distance, stack one-HVL lead slabs until the boundary post clears 2 mR/h, then plan a job dose with the time slider.

tasks.label: Double your distance and watch the rate fall 4x
tasks.hint: Inverse square: I₁d₁² = I₂d₂² — twice the distance spreads the same photons over four times the area.

tasks.label: Stack lead slabs until the boundary post reads 2 mR/h
tasks.hint: Each slab is one HVL and halves the rate; about 3.3 HVL make one TVL — count the halvings needed from ~230 mR/h down to 2.

tasks.label: Plan a 10-minute job that stays under 20 mrem
tasks.hint: Dose = rate × time — trade distance and shielding against the clock; ALARA is all three levers pulled at once.
